title: Ethanol mutual funds: Experts suggest how investors can explore this new theme and key risks to watch
summary: Experts say there are no pure-play ethanol mutual funds, but investors can gain exposure through select index, thematic and sectoral funds. Given the cyclical, regulatory and adoption risks, they suggest diversified funds rather than concentrated stock bets.

title: ₹60 lakh salary: Here is how to calculate final tax liability after surcharge under new tax regime
summary: A ₹60 lakh salary attracts a surcharge of 10% under the new tax regime in addition to slab-wise income tax and 4% health and education cess. In this case, the final tax liability comes to ₹15.53 lakh, resulting in an effective tax rate of 25.88%.

title: Moved back to India? This buffer can help you with tax planning
summary: During the RNOR window, your Indian income is taxed exactly as it would be for any resident. Any income earned in India is taxable and must be declared in the ITR

title: EPS pension 2026: How much monthly pension can you get after 10 years of service?
summary: The EPS Pension 2026 replaces previous schemes and retains the core pension formula. Employees who need a monthly pension must have 10 years of service and retire at 58. The estimated monthly pension after 10 years is ₹2,143, with a minimum pension floor of ₹1,000.

title: Bengaluru corporate couple ‘accidentally started saving ₹30,000 a month’ after relocating to Manali to work remotely
summary: A Bengaluru corporate couple relocated to Manali to work remotely. They shared their experience detailing how they accidentally started saving ₹30,000 a month.

title: Exclusive holdings: Stocks owned by just one mutual fund scheme, with no other scheme holding them
summary: Value Research data shows that 13 stocks are owned by only one mutual fund scheme each. Such exclusive holdings offer an insight into the differentiated investment strategies adopted by some fund managers.

title: NPS update: Government extends 2 new investment options to these employees
summary: The Finance Ministry has extended two additional investment choices under the National Pension System to employees of central autonomous bodies. Eligible subscribers can now opt for pension funds with equity exposure of up to 75%, depending on their risk appetite.

title: Can delayed home construction cost you Section 54 relief? ITAT answers
summary: Sold a house and reinvested the gains but missed the construction deadline due to Covid? The Chennai ITAT says that alone is not enough to deny Section 54 tax exemption. The Tribunal held that pandemic-related delays cannot be treated as negligence and allowed the capital gains exemption claim.

title: Paying over ₹50,000 in monthly rent? Here's why you must deduct TDS before making payment to your landlord
summary: Individuals paying monthly rent above ₹50,000 are required to deduct TDS at 2% before making the payment to the landlord. Timely deduction, deposit of TDS through Form 26QC and issuance of Form 16C can help avoid unnecessary penalties and tax notices.
